title: Cacalia hirtiflora
taxonomy: Cacalia hirtiflora is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places Cacalia hirtiflora alongside other flowering plants that share similar characteristics. The genus Cacalia is a small group of flowering plants, with Cacalia hirtiflora being a unique species within this group. Further classification within the Asteraceae family is into the tribe Cacaliae, which comprises a small number of closely related species. This detailed classification provides insight into the evolutionary relationships between different plant species.
uses:
Cacalia hirtiflora is a plant species native to East Asia, and its medicinal uses are still being studied. 
Cacalia hirtiflora has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. 
Studies on the plant have shown that it may have antimicrobial and antiviral effects, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues. 
Cacalia hirtiflora may also have a role in protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage, which can contribute to aging and certain diseases. 
More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Cacalia hirtiflora and its potential applications in human health.
